overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions unanimously and forwarded an amended fee waiver resolution to the county board that will only cover septic reconnect fees for tornado-affected properties. The committee also received department overviews from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing: Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential) - Town of Green Valley
Public hearing: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (conservancy recreation to general agriculture) - Town of McMillan
Consideration of fee waivers for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Department overview: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry Department
Department overview: Conservation, Planning, and Zoning Department

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes with Supervisor Drebeck seconding. The motion carried unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition - Town of Green Valley
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential for a proposed land division. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered for, against, or as interested. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact, conclusions of law, and town approval; Supervisor Kurt seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition - Town of McMillan
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from conservancy recreation to general agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature, and Pogle explained the property contains a home and horse riding stable - the rezoning would make these conforming uses rather than non-conforming. No natural areas would be lost. Town of McMillan recommended approval April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefelt moved to approve; Supervisor Drew seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waivers for April 17, 2026 severe weather impacted properties
discussions.body: Director Lori Mskimmons presented staff recommendations to waive county zoning and septic fees for properties damaged by the April 17th tornado in Ringle, Kronenwetter, Eastston, and Weston. Significant debate occurred with Supervisor Drew questioning fairness for future smaller disasters, Supervisor Leur asking about budget impacts, and Supervisor Drebeck expressing concerns about precedent. Eric Vogel asked if homeowners insurance would cover fees. Administrator Lance Leonhard emphasized this was triggered by the governor's emergency declaration. Supervisor Sefelt moved to forward the resolution; Supervisor Leur seconded. Supervisor Drebeck then amended the motion to limit fee waivers to only septic reconnect fees ($160), excluding all other zoning and pouts fees. Supervisor K seconded the amendment. The amendment carried. The amended motion then carried, though not unanimously.

David Hagenbuscher presented Solid Waste overview covering landfill operations, the new renewable natural gas partnership with Vidi Energy, leachate treatment challenges requiring $6 million in capital projects, and PFAS regulations requiring treatment by 2032. Tom Lovelin presented Parks, Recreation and Forestry overview noting the department serves both city and county, operates 17 county parks and 30,700 acres of county forest, and that Nine Mile generates $3.25 million for the local economy. Supervisor Sefelt announced Tom Lovelin is retiring June 1st after nearly 30 years of service.

actionItems: Rezoning petition for James Offer and David Tract approved - forwarded to County Board
Rezoning petition for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz approved - forwarded to County Board
Amended fee waiver resolution (limited to septic reconnect fees only) forwarded to County Board for consideration
CPZ staff to track costs and requests related to tornado fee waivers for future policy development
Joe Tucker selected as new County Forest Administrator to replace retiring Tom Lovelin
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
